the city council tonight In connec
ton with the members of the union
depot committee and citizens. The
draft of the agreement with the
Great Northern as to its -connec
ton with the Spokane, Portland &
Seattle will also be considered.


The Spokane banks say that they
will have an abundance of silver tc
handle the holiday trade. The
only scarcity in silver has been in
silver dollars; there is an abun
dance of halves, quarters, dimes
and nickles.


Leon Foose, the fireman who hac
his leg badly injured in the wreck
at Lind, has gone to his home a,
Cunningham, where he will be con
fined for some time.


The Spokane lodge Of the Sons
of Norway rendered an enthusias
tic reception Sunday night to Hans
Bugge of Bellingham, president Of
the national association. A cam
paign to Increase the membership
was urged at the meeting.
A convention Of the c. w. B. NJ
of the north end of the East Wash
ington district will be held tomor
row at the Pacific Avenue Chris
tian church, Cook and Pacific.


r. e. Strahorn, president of the
North Coast railway, has gone tc
North Yakima with H. j. Pierce, q
New Yorker interested in the Stra
horn ventures.


The chamber Of commerce com
mittee on military affairs is much
pleased with the result of efforts
to secure funds for the equipment
of a gymnasium, purchase marks
manship prizes and equipment for
the drum corps, which will be a
feature of the Spokane militia
within a short time. Seven hun
dred dollars has been promised
with more in sight.


Believing that the water from
Egbert springs could be used to
better advantage for irrigation pur
poses than to feed the thirst of the
Great Northern locomotives, w. M
Tolliver shut off the railroad water
supply.


Yesterday Judge J. Stanley Web
ster sentenced G. M. Whitson, the
forger, to from one to 20 years
in Walla Walla.


The United States authorities
have failed to discover clue to
the identity of the person or per.
sons who have circulated number
of bogus SIS and $40 bills in the
northwest. The supposition is
that they were made in Italy and
brought to this country by
laborers.


Corporation Counsel Conner and
Danson & Williams have filed a
motion asking that the $4,000
award in the Engelking case be
set aside on account of irregulari
